## Freelance Service Agreement (Brand Refresh 2025)

### Preamble

This Freelance Service Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of 2025-01-15 ("Effective Date") by and between TechStartup Inc., through its Marketing Director Sarah Johnson ("Client"), and Chen Creative Studio, through Senior Designer Alex Chen ("Freelancer"). Freelancer will perform design services for Client under the terms set forth herein for the project entitled "Brand Refresh 2025."

### Recitals

WHEREAS, Client desires to retain Freelancer to perform a complete visual identity redesign including logo, color palette, typography, and a brand guidelines document; and WHEREAS, Freelancer has the skills, experience and qualifications to provide such services and deliverables; NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and promises contained herein, the parties agree as follows.

---

## Clauses (13 total)

### DEFINITIONS

*Type: standard*

1.1 "Services": The design, development, consultation and related efforts provided by Freelancer for the Brand Refresh 2025 project, including meetings, revisions, and production of Deliverables.
1.2 "Deliverables": The items specifically to be delivered by Freelancer to Client under this Agreement: logo concepts, selected logo refinements, brand guidelines document, and asset package delivery, each as described in Clause 2.
1.3 "Final Deliverables": The final versions of Deliverables delivered to Client after acceptance and completion of revision rounds and any agreed post-production, prepared for Client's use as specified in this Agreement.
1.4 "Client Materials": Any materials, content, data, trademark or design guidance, and other items provided by Client to Freelancer to assist in the performance of the Services.
1.5 "Freelancer Tools": Freelancer's pre-existing design tools, templates, software, methodologies, processes, fonts, code, or other tangible or intangible materials used or developed by Freelancer in connection with providing the Services, excluding Client Materials and Final Deliverables specifically created for Client.
1.6 "Work Product": All original works of authorship, deliverables, drafts, modifications and other results created by Freelancer in the course of performing the Services, including Final Deliverables, but excluding Freelancer Tools and Preliminary Works.

### SERVICES, SCOPE AND DELIVERABLES

*Type: standard*

2.1 Services: Freelancer will provide Services for the Brand Refresh 2025 project as described in this Clause and Schedule.
2.2 Project Description: Complete visual identity redesign including logo, color palette, typography, and brand guidelines document.
2.3 Deliverables, Due Dates and Payment Allocation:
  2.3.1 Logo concepts (3 options) — due Jan 30, 2025 — $2,000.
  2.3.2 Selected logo refinement — due Feb 15, 2025 — $1,500.
  2.3.3 Brand guidelines document — due Mar 1, 2025 — $2,500.
  2.3.4 Asset package delivery — due Mar 15, 2025 — $2,500.
2.4 Deadline: Final Deliverables to be completed and delivered by 2025-03-15.
2.5 Revisions: Freelancer will include up to three (3) rounds of revisions for each Deliverable as reasonably described and limited to the scope of the selected concept; additional revisions will be billed at Freelancer's standard hourly rate upon prior written approval.

### PAYMENT AND PAYMENT SCHEDULE

*Type: standard*

3.1 Total Amount: Client agrees to pay Freelancer a total fee of $8,500 for the Services.
3.2 Deposit: Client shall pay an initial deposit of $2,550 due upon signing.
3.3 Milestone Payment Schedule: Payments are due upon completion/approval of each milestone as follows:
  3.3.1 Upon delivery of Logo concepts (due Jan 30, 2025): $2,000.
  3.3.2 Upon delivery of Selected logo refinement (due Feb 15, 2025): $1,500.
  3.3.3 Upon delivery of Brand guidelines document (due Mar 1, 2025): $2,500.
  3.3.4 Upon delivery of Asset package (due Mar 15, 2025): $2,500.
3.4 Invoicing and Payment Terms: Freelancer will invoice Client for each milestone amount. Invoices are payable within fifteen (15) days of receipt. Late payments accrue interest at 1.5% per month or the maximum allowed by law, whichever is less.
3.5 Taxes: Client is responsible for any sales, use, VAT or other taxes applicable to the Services, excluding Freelancer's income taxes.

### KILL FEE; TERMINATION FOR CONVENIENCE

*Type: standard*

4.1 Termination for Convenience: Client may terminate this Agreement for convenience after Freelancer has begun work upon written notice to Freelancer.
4.2 Kill Fee: If Client terminates for convenience after work has begun, Client will pay Freelancer a kill fee equal to ten percent (10%) of the unpaid amount then due under this Agreement, subject to a maximum kill fee of $850. For purposes of this Clause, "unpaid amount" means the Total Amount less amounts already paid by Client at the time of termination.
4.3 Payment Timing: The kill fee is due within fifteen (15) days of Client's notice of termination for convenience.
4.4 Termination for Cause: Either party may terminate for material breach by the other party if the breach is not cured within fourteen (14) days after written notice specifying the breach.

### INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY, MORAL RIGHTS, AND PORTFOLIO USE

*Type: standard*

5.1 Ownership of Final Deliverables: Upon full payment of all amounts due under this Agreement, Freelancer assigns to Client all right, title and interest in and to the Final Deliverables, subject to the limited exceptions and Freelancer Tools identified herein.
5.2 Freelancer Tools and Preliminary Works: Freelancer retains ownership of Freelancer Tools and any unused concepts, sketches, preliminary works, or other Preliminary Works developed during the project that are not included in the Final Deliverables. Client is granted a license to any Preliminary Works only if and to the extent such items are incorporated into Final Deliverables.
5.3 Moral Rights: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, Freelancer hereby irrevocably waives and agrees not to assert any moral rights, droit moral, or similar rights in the Final Deliverables against Client or its successors and assigns. If any such waiver is not permitted by law, Freelancer agrees to execute any documents or take any steps reasonably requested by Client to effectuate a waiver or transfer of such rights.
5.4 Portfolio Use: Freelancer may display the Deliverables and Final Deliverables in Freelancer's portfolio, website, social media and promotional materials after Client's public launch of the new brand, provided that such display does not disclose Client Materials designated by Client as confidential or otherwise subject to a prior written objection by Client for bona fide confidentiality reasons. If Client notifies Freelancer in writing prior to public launch that certain Deliverables are confidential, Freelancer will refrain from public display of those items until Client authorizes.
5.5 Trademark Disclaimer: Client is responsible for clearance and use of any trademarks, trade names or service marks. Freelancer provides design and creative services only and does not provide legal advice; Freelancer disclaims any responsibility for trademark clearance, registration, infringement or suitability for Client's intended use.

### WARRANTIES; PRELIMINARY WORKS; INFRINGEMENT REMEDIES

*Type: standard*

6.1 Freelancer Warranty: Freelancer warrants that the Services shall be performed in a professional and workmanlike manner in accordance with industry standards and that the Final Deliverables will be original to Freelancer except to the extent based on Client Materials or third-party materials provided by Client.
6.2 Client Warranty: Client warrants that Client Materials provided to Freelancer do not infringe the rights of third parties and that Client has the right to grant Freelancer any licenses necessary to use Client Materials.
6.3 Preliminary Works: Concepts, sketches, mockups or other Preliminary Works created by Freelancer that are not selected as Final Deliverables remain the property of Freelancer and may be used by Freelancer in other projects.
6.4 Indemnification Remedies for Infringement Claims: If a third-party claim arises alleging that the Final Deliverables infringe a third party's intellectual property rights, Freelancer's sole and exclusive obligation and Client's sole and exclusive remedy, subject to Freelancer's indemnification obligations in this Agreement, will be at Freelancer's election and expense to: (i) procure for Client the right to continue using the allegedly infringing Final Deliverable; or (ii) replace or modify the allegedly infringing Final Deliverable so that it becomes non-infringing while maintaining substantially similar functionality and appearance; or (iii) if options (i) and (ii) are not commercially reasonable, refund to Client the fees paid for the specific Deliverable that is the subject of the claim. The remedies in this Clause are conditioned on Client (a) providing Freelancer prompt written notice of the claim, (b) permitting Freelancer to control the defense and settlement of the claim, and (c) reasonably cooperating at Freelancer's expense. This Clause states the entire liability of Freelancer for infringement of third-party intellectual property rights.

### CONFIDENTIALITY

*Type: standard*

7.1 Confidential Information: Each party ("Receiving Party") shall keep confidential the other party's ("Disclosing Party") confidential information disclosed in connection with the Services and shall not disclose such information to any third party except as permitted hereunder. Confidential Information does not include information that is publicly known through no fault of Receiving Party, independently developed by Receiving Party, rightfully received from a third party without obligation of confidentiality, or required to be disclosed by law.
7.2 Use Limitation: Receiving Party shall use Confidential Information solely for the purpose of performing its obligations under this Agreement.
7.3 Return or Destruction: Upon termination or written request, Receiving Party will return or destroy Confidential Information of Disclosing Party, except that Freelancer may retain copies of Final Deliverables and Freelancer Tools as necessary for tax, billing, insurance or portfolio use as permitted by this Agreement.

### INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR; SUBCONTRACTING

*Type: standard*

8.1 Independent Contractor: The parties acknowledge that Freelancer is an independent contractor and not an employee, agent, joint venturer, or partner of Client.
8.2 Subcontracting: Freelancer may, in Freelancer's discretion, engage qualified subcontractors to perform portions of the Services provided that Freelancer remains responsible for the performance of Services and compliance with this Agreement. Freelancer will ensure subcontractors are bound by confidentiality obligations consistent with this Agreement.

### ASSIGNMENT

*Type: standard*

9.1 General Restriction: Neither party may assign or transfer this Agreement or any of its rights or obligations hereunder without the prior written consent of the other party, except as provided in Clause 9.2.
9.2 Permitted Assignment: Notwithstanding Clause 9.1, either party may assign this Agreement without consent in connection with a merger, acquisition, corporate reorganization or sale of substantially all of its assets, provided that the assignee assumes the assigning party's obligations under this Agreement in writing.

### LIMITATION OF LIABILITY

*Type: standard*

10.1 Exclusion of Consequential Damages: Except for a party's willful misconduct or gross negligence, in no event will either party be liable for special, incidental, consequential, punitive or exemplary damages, including loss of profits, revenue or business opportunity, even if advised of the possibility of such damages.
10.2 Limitation of Liability: Except for liability arising from (a) gross negligence or willful misconduct, (b) Client's payment obligations, or (c) Freelancer's breach of confidentiality or indemnification obligations for third-party claims, the aggregate liability of either party for any claim arising out of or relating to this Agreement will not exceed the total amount of fees actually paid by Client to Freelancer under this Agreement.

### TERM, TERMINATION AND EFFECT OF TERMINATION

*Type: standard*

11.1 Term: This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues until completion of the Services and delivery of Final Deliverables, unless earlier terminated in accordance with this Agreement.
11.2 Effect of Termination: Upon termination, Client will pay Freelancer for Services performed and Deliverables delivered through the effective date of termination and any applicable kill fee under Clause 4. Freelancer will deliver to Client any Final Deliverables completed and any Client Materials in Freelancer's possession upon receipt of payment for the same.
11.3 Survival: Provisions that by their nature survive termination (including but not limited to payment obligations, ownership, confidentiality, limitation of liability, indemnity, and governing law) will survive termination or expiration of this Agreement.

### INDEMNIFICATION

*Type: standard*

12.1 Freelancer Indemnity: Freelancer will indemnify, defend and hold Client harmless from and against any third-party claims alleging that Freelancer's unmodified Final Deliverables infringe a third party's intellectual property rights, subject to the remedies and limitations in Clause 6.4, except where infringement arises from Client Materials, Client's instructions, or uses not contemplated by this Agreement.
12.2 Client Indemnity: Client will indemnify, defend and hold Freelancer harmless from and against any third-party claims arising from Client Materials, Client's use of the Final Deliverables beyond the scope of this Agreement, or Client's breach of warranties herein.
12.3 Indemnification Procedure: The indemnified party will provide prompt written notice of any claim, and the indemnifying party shall control the defense and settlement of the claim, provided that the indemnified party may participate at its own expense and that the indemnifying party will not settle any claim that imposes any obligation or admission of liability on the indemnified party without the indemnified party's prior written consent.

### MISCELLANEOUS

*Type: standard*

13.1 Governing Law and Jurisdiction: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, USA, without regard to its conflict of law principles. The parties submit to the exclusive jurisdiction and venue of the state and federal courts located in California for any dispute arising out of or relating to this Agreement.
13.2 Notices: All notices under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be deemed given when delivered personally, sent by nationally recognized overnight courier, or three (3) business days after deposit in the U.S. mail, postage prepaid, to the addresses provided by the parties.
13.3 Amendment: This Agreement may be amended only by a written instrument signed by both parties.
13.4 Severability: If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions will remain in full force and effect.
13.5 Entire Agreement: This Agreement, together with any exhibits or attachments referenced herein, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties regarding the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, proposals and communications, whether written or oral.
13.6 Counterparts; Electronic Signatures: This Agreement may be executed in counterparts and by electronic signature, each of which will be deemed an original but all of which together will constitute one and the same instrument.
